description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with visible wear and discoloration, suggesting frequent use. The head is forged from metal, showing a broad, wedge-shaped blade with a slightly convex edge and a thick poll opposite the cutting edge. The blade is securely mounted onto the handle using a traditional wedged fitting, with the metal extending slightly above the wood. The metal head displays a weathered, matte finish with some surface rust and patina, indicating age and exposure to the elements. The handle has a natural wood grain and a smooth, ergonomic contour for grip, with no additional wrapping or rubberized sections. There are faint markings or symbols on the side of the blade, but no clear manufacturer label is visible.
description: A weathered hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, metal wedge-shaped blade, showing signs of age and use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the curved wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped metal blade with faint markings.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the thickness of the blade and the way it is wedged into the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the full face of the blade, with visible wear on both materials.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the alignment of the blade with the handle and the secure mounting style.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with an oval cross-section, smooth surface, and ergonomic grip; no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped steel blade with a convex edge and thick poll; no beard, with faint markings on the side.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fitting with the blade head inserted and secured onto the wooden handle.
